feature_name: Dual-mode operation: Root Complex and End Point
description: The controller supports both Root Complex (RC) and End Point (EP) roles in a single Dual Mode core. Mode selection is via bootstrap pins PCIESSMODE[1:0] and may be overridden by software. As RC, it performs downstream configuration, address decoding, and forwarding; as EP, it presents BARs and responds to RC configuration. The module maps application registers into BAR0 in both modes to allow management over PCIe. Initialization sequences are provided for each role, including link enable and enumeration steps.
evidence: Section 1.4.1; Section 2.11.1/2.11.2 Initialization Sequence; Registers CMD_STATUS[LTSSM_EN] (3.1.3), BIST_HEADER[HDR_TYPE] (3.3.2/3.4.2)
dependencies: SerDes clock/PLL locked; device power domain enabled; correct PCIESSMODE strap or software override
Remarks: Choose mode early in boot; ensure RC/EP appropriate BAR and capability programming before enabling link.

feature_name: Link width and speed: x1/x2, Gen1/Gen2 with dynamic width and lane reversal
description: The controller supports single-port bidirectional links with up to x2 lanes and Gen1 (2.5 GT/s) and Gen2 (5.0 GT/s) speeds. Hardware provides automatic lane reversal and dynamic-width conversion. Speed change to Gen2 after Gen1 bring-up is controlled via PL_GEN2[DIR_SPD]. Physical de-emphasis, swing, and number of FTS are configurable for link tuning. Negotiated speed and width are readable in LINK_STAT_CTRL.
evidence: Features (1.3); PL_GEN2[DIR_SPD, DEEMPH, CFG_TX_SWING, NUM_FTS] (3.9.13); LINK_STAT_CTRL[LINK_SPEED, NEGOTIATED_LINK_WD] (3.7.6)
dependencies: Reference clock quality; SerDes configuration; common clock settings if SSC/common-clock used
Remarks: Hardware negotiates final parameters, but drivers should program Gen2 enable and review negotiated values for policy.

feature_name: Address Translation: Outbound (32 regions) and Inbound (4 regions)
description: Outbound translation divides a CPU address window into 32 equal regions of 1/2/4/8 MB and remaps to PCIe addresses. It uses OB_SIZE, OB_OFFSET_INDEXn/OB_OFFSETn_HI and region enable bits; transactions violating region boundaries are undefined. Inbound translation provides four regions keyed by BAR match (IB_BARn) that map incoming PCIe to internal addresses using start and offset registers. BAR0 is an exception and always maps to application registers. 32-bit and 64-bit addressing scenarios are supported and documented with examples.
dependencies: Enable CMD_STATUS[OB_XLT_EN]/[IB_XLT_EN]; correctly sized CPU window; BAR programming by RC
Remarks: Carefully size regions to avoid crossing boundaries; in EP designs preconfigure BAR masks via DBI_CS2 before enumeration.

feature_name: Configuration address spaces and register access windows
description: Address Space 0 (16 KB) hosts application registers, local config, remote config and, in RC, a 4 KB I/O TLP window. Address Space 1 is the data transfer window mapped by BARs/translation. Application registers are always reachable via BAR0 from PCIe. Remote configuration accesses are performed by programming CFG_SETUP then using local config space offsets. These windows do not support bursts and must be accessed as non-cacheable 32-bit transactions.
dependencies: Operational link required for remote accesses; correct CFG_SETUP programming
Remarks: Gate remote config/IO accesses on link-up to avoid timeouts; treat windows as strongly-ordered, non-cacheable.

feature_name: Endian conversion for inbound and outbound traffic
description: While the PCIe core is little-endian at the pins, the subsystem supports big-endian swizzling for both inbound and outbound transactions. ENDIAN register selects swap granularity on 1/2/4/8-byte boundaries to match system memory layout. Big-endian conversion applies to data and corresponding byte enables for accurate partial writes. For outbound non-prefetchable/MMR 32-bit accesses, conversion is fixed on 32-bit boundaries. Proper configuration avoids data misinterpretation across heterogeneous systems.
dependencies: System endianness setting at boot; consistent driver programming of ENDIAN
Remarks: Establish a single policy early; verify with test patterns and unaligned/partial writes during bring-up.

feature_name: Interrupt architecture: Legacy INTx and MSI generation/reception
description: The module exposes up to 14 interrupt sources to the SoC and supports EP legacy INTx assertion/deassertion and MSI generation. As RC, it receives downstream INTx and MSI and aggregates events with status/enable and EOI semantics. MSI is generated by EP via programmed memory writes to RC MSI_IRQ with vector encoding, and received into MSIn_* blocks with per-vector enables and W1C status. Additional PM/reset/error interrupt blocks provide link-down, PM events, and transaction error signaling. EOI writes are required to complete interrupt servicing.
dependencies: Correct capability programming (MSI_CAP.MSI_EN, INTx disable), BAR0 reachability; CPU interrupt controller mapping
Remarks: Coalesce where possible; clear status with W1C then write IRQ_EOI; document vector mapping between PCIe and CPU IRQ lines.

feature_name: Power management: Device D-states and Link L0s/L1 with ASPM
description: Device D0/D1/D2/D3hot/D3cold states are supported per PCIe PM capability, with PME generation options. Link ASPM supports L0s and L1, with software and hardware-driven entry/exit latencies configurable via Link and ACK_FREQ registers. The relationship between device and link states is defined to ensure coherent transitions. L2/L3 Ready and L2 are described, with L2 not supported by this module. PM and reset interrupts notify software of PME, turnoff, and link reset requests.
evidence: Sections 2.15.x; PMCAP/PM_CTL_STAT (3.5.2/3.5.3); LINK_STAT_CTRL[ACTIVE_LINK_PM, COMMON_CLK_CFG] (3.7.6); ACK_FREQ (3.9.5); PMRST_* (3.1.76–3.1.79)
dependencies: Common clock policy; platform PM constraints; PME routing
Remarks: Tune L0s/L1 timers to balance latency and power; ensure D3->D0 soft reset policy matches firmware expectations.

feature_name: Clocking and SerDes PLL bring-up with SSC/common clock options
description: The PHY relies on a high-quality differential reference clock (typically 100 MHz) to generate the internal SerDes PLL bit clock. Driver must program PCIE_SERDES_CFGPLL (MPY, LB, ENPLL) and poll PCIE_SERDES_STS[LOCK] before accessing PCIe registers. Common-clock versus separate-clock operation impacts training sequences and should be reflected by LINK_STAT_CTRL[COMMON_CLK_CFG]. Spread Spectrum Clocking requires common clock. Access to SerDes config uses the device-level kicker to unlock chip-level registers.
dependencies: Reference clock routing; kicker unlock sequence; power domain enabled
Remarks: Gate all PCIe access on PLL lock; document SSC usage; set COMMON_CLK_CFG appropriately to minimize training retries.

feature_name: Reset model: POR, system reset, and software hot reset
description: The module supports hardware power-on reset (cold), system warm reset, and a software-initiated hot reset downstream. RSTCMD[INIT_RST] requests a downstream hot reset and RSTCMD[FLUSH_N] reflects pending transactions. Soft reset leaves sticky bits and PM logic intact. After any reset, software must delay before issuing PCIe transactions per spec timing. Power domain on/off transitions fully reset the block and require link re-initialization.
dependencies: Ensure all DMA/bus transactions quiesced; delay per spec (>=100 ms) before accesses
Remarks: Sequence: quiesce -> check FLUSH_N=0 -> issue reset -> wait -> re-init link -> re-enumerate as needed.

feature_name: Loopback support: PIPE and PHY loopback modes
description: PIPE loopback allows master/slave symbol-level testing with link partner or test equipment; control via PL_LINK_CTRL[LPBK_EN] and retrain/force link. PHY loopback routes TX to RX internally for TLP loopback without a partner, configured via SERDES_CFGx[TX/RX_LOOPBACK] and forcing LTSSM states. Additional constraints include disabling BAR conflicts and ensuring not to use config TLPs in RC when in PHY loopback. Procedures are provided for KeyStone I devices with forced POLL_ACTIVE entry.
dependencies: SerDes configured and link disabled or forced to recovery; correct LTSSM forcing
Remarks: Use for production test and bring-up; avoid generating config TLPs in RC PHY loopback; restore normal settings after tests.

feature_name: Error handling: PCI, baseline PCIe, and Advanced Error Reporting (AER)
description: The controller supports PCI-compatible error bits, PCIe baseline error reporting, and AER extended capabilities. Software can enable/disable, mask, and classify correctable/non-fatal/fatal errors, and log offending TLP headers (HDR_LOG0–3). Root ports can route SERR and PME as interrupts and record source IDs. ECRC generation/check is supported but should be disabled if link partners do not support it, especially in RC mode. Comprehensive status and W1C semantics are provided for robust recovery.
evidence: Sections 2.16.x; STATUS_COMMAND (3.2.3); DEV_STAT_CTRL (3.7.4); PCIE_UNCERR/CERR/_MASK/_SVRTY (3.8.3–3.8.7); PCIE_ACCR (3.8.8); ROOT_ERR_* (3.8.13/3.8.14)
dependencies: Consistent capability init across endpoints; error ISR routing; ECRC policy
Remarks: Adopt a clear policy for error class handling and escalation; log headers promptly and clear W1C bits; disable ECRC if peers lack support.

feature_name: Transaction and alignment requirements with byte strobe rules
description: Internal bus transaction size is limited to 128 bytes, with stricter 120-byte limit if start address is not 8-byte aligned. Inbound reads crossing 128-byte boundaries may be split, and inbound TLPs cannot cross 4 KB boundaries by spec. Only incremental burst type is supported; fixed/wrap bursts may lead to bus lockup. Byte enables must be a single contiguous 1s run for writes; holes are disallowed. Observing these rules prevents protocol and interconnect errors.
dependencies: DMA engine configuration; software memcpy/DMA routines respecting limits
Remarks: Program DMA aCnt/bCnt to payload boundaries and alignment; validate software copy paths for contiguous byte enables.

feature_name: TLP attribute control: Relaxed Ordering and No Snoop
description: Global control of RO and No Snoop attributes for outgoing TLPs is provided. TLPCFG[RELAXED] enables relaxed ordering for all TLPs, and TLPCFG[NO_SNOOP] marks them as non-snoop. RC can additionally control DEV_STAT_CTRL[RELAXED] behavior for received traffic ordering. These knobs allow tuning performance versus ordering guarantees across the hierarchy. Misuse can lead to visibility or coherency surprises in multi-master systems.
evidence: TLPCFG (3.1.6); DEV_STAT_CTRL[RELAXED] (3.7.4); Section 2.14.5 note on relaxed ordering
dependencies: Platform coherency/ordering model; downstream device support
Remarks: Enable selectively by queue/class; disable for control/doorbells; document ordering assumptions for software components.

feature_name: DMA usage model over VBUSM master/slave ports (no internal DMA)
description: The PCIe block exposes a 128-bit VBUSM slave port for outbound transfers and a 128-bit master port for inbound accesses. There is no internal DMA; system DMA (e.g., EDMA) must be used to drive high throughput on the slave side. Bandwidth targets are ~85% of 2.0 Gbps per lane at Gen1 and ~85% of 4.0 Gbps per lane at Gen2 in each direction, assuming well-sized payloads. Examples are provided for EDMA memory write/read setup with payload-sized bursts. Address translation can be used to map convenient CPU windows for each remote target.
dependencies: Working EDMA or equivalent; outbound translation programmed; bus arbitration budgets
Remarks: Size aCnt to payload and bCnt to buffer; avoid misaligned starts; ensure interconnect QoS is provisioned for target bandwidth.

feature_name: BAR Mask overlay and pre-enumeration BAR sizing
description: EP firmware can predefine BAR sizes and types by writing BAR Mask registers overlaid on BARs when DBI_CS2 is set. This allows advertising exact resource needs before RC enumeration, including 64-bit BAR pairs. BAR0 mask is limited (bits [31:20]) because BAR0 is dedicated to application registers. Writes are write-only, and verification is by programming BARs with test patterns to see masked bits. DBI_CS2 must be cleared before enumeration proceeds.
dependencies: DBI_CS2 gating; BAR enable bits set; correct test-and-verify sequence
Remarks: Do not modify masks from PCIe side; perform write-back verification; remember BAR0 always maps registers.

feature_name: Diagnostics and bring-up aids: LTSSM debug and CRC injection
description: DEBUG0/1 expose LTSSM state, training signals, and link idles to aid bring-up and failure triage. Appendix A provides encoded-to-name mapping for all LTSSM states. DIAG_CTRL allows single-packet LCRC/ECRC inversion to validate error paths and AER handling. Activity status flags indicate inbound/outbound buffer occupancy for safe reset or low-power entry. Together, these tools reduce lab time and clarify link issues.
dependencies: Operational access to application registers; AER enabled when injecting
Remarks: Use during bring-up and CI tests; clear injected errors and verify ISR paths; log LTSSM transitions around failures.

feature_name: Programmable symbol/timer parameters and filter masks
description: Symbol/timer registers let software adjust ACK/Nak, replay, SKP intervals, and training symbol counts. Filter masks control acceptance or treatment of specific TLP/DLLP cases (e.g., ECRC errors, vendor messages, locked reads) primarily for test and corner-case handling. These controls can relax or tighten strictness during evaluation or interoperability testing. Defaults are spec-compliant and suitable for production. Misconfiguration may degrade reliability.
dependencies: Understanding of PCIe timing; stable link partners
Remarks: Change only under controlled test; record deviations from defaults; revert to defaults for field firmware.

feature_name: Initialization sequences and APP_RETRY_EN for slow bring-up
description: Formal sequences for RC and EP cover SerDes enable, LTSSM gating, configuration, and training. During EP bring-up, APP_RETRY_EN can force Retry responses to delay host timeouts while local configuration completes. Drivers must poll DEBUG0[LTSSM_STATE]==L0 to confirm link-up before accessing remote devices. After RC link-up, enumerate downstream, assign BARs, and configure DMA/interrupt protocols. For EP, wait for RC to program BARs and capabilities before initiating outbound requests.
dependencies: SerDes lock achieved; power domain on; correct strap/mode
Remarks: Use Retry gating sparingly; clear before normal operation; always gate remote/IO accesses on link-up.

feature_name: PHY/PCS configuration and status (KeyStone I)
description: PCS and SerDes per-lane configuration registers provide control of loopback, EQ, CDR, polarity, and status. PCS supports options like PIPE 1.86 behavior, short timers for debug, and RX/TX enable states. Status registers report lane enables, TX/RX on, and PCS revision. These are only available on KeyStone I devices; KeyStone II uses a separate SerDes user guide. Correct RX_LOS enable value is 0x4 per errata note in SPRUGS6D.
evidence: SERDES_CFG0/1 (3.1.101/102); PCS_CFG0/1/STATUS (3.1.98–3.1.100); Release notes page ii
dependencies: Device family differentiation; kicker unlock for chip-level SerDes regs
Remarks: Mirror lane settings across used lanes; apply documented corrections (RX_LOS=0x4) when configuring receivers.

feature_name: I/O transactions from RC via 4 KB IO window
description: In RC mode only, a 4 KB internal window maps to outbound I/O space accesses. IOBASE holds the 20 MSBs of the target IO address and all accesses in the window generate IO TLPs. Inbound I/O accesses are not supported in either RC or EP modes. The IO window is intended for legacy device support and should be used for 32-bit aligned dword transactions only. Misuse can result in unsupported request completions.
dependencies: RC mode; correct IOBASE programming; link up
Remarks: Prefer memory-mapped accesses where possible; ensure alignment and size constraints are met for IO TLPs.

feature_name: Message and Other Message support
description: The controller can generate and receive PCIe Messages, including PM and error-related messages. The PL_OMSG register allows software to transmit custom message payloads when OMSG_REQ is strobed. Root ports can enable PME interrupt delivery via Root Control and Capabilities. Message filtering, including vendor-specific messages, is configurable through filter masks. These mechanisms enable standards-compliant control-plane interactions.
dependencies: Correct routing IDs and capabilities; message class enables
Remarks: Use sparingly for control paths; test interoperability with switches/hosts that rate-limit or drop unsupported messages.
